## Title: Fix missed scheduling opportunity in ESM Status message handler

### Summary
The ESM Status message handler in the UE NAS stack was not triggering scheduling for pending data after processing status messages. When the UE had pending data to send on active bearers following an ESM Status exchange, the lower layers were not notified, causing a missed scheduling opportunity and potential data transmission delays.

This patch modifies the `esm_recv_status()` function to accept a `nas_user_t` parameter and checks for pending data on active bearers after successful status processing. If pending data is detected, the function sets the scheduling request flag (`sched_req`) to ensure the UE is properly scheduled for uplink transmission. This ensures that data transmission is not stalled after ESM status procedures.

### Changes
- `openair3/NAS/UE/ESM/SAP/esm_recv.c`: Modified `esm_recv_status()` to accept `nas_user_t *user` parameter and added logic to check for pending data on active bearers, setting `sched_req` flag when needed
- `openair3/NAS/UE/ESM/SAP/esm_recv.h`: Updated function prototype for `esm_recv_status()` to include `nas_user_t *user` parameter
- `openair3/NAS/UE/ESM/SAP/esm_sap.c`: Updated call site to pass the `user` parameter when calling `esm_recv_status()`

### Testing
- Verified that UE with pending data is properly scheduled after ESM Status message exchange
- Tested scenarios with multiple active bearers to ensure correct scheduling request generation
- Confirmed no regression in existing ESM status handling procedures

   136	 **                                                                        **
   137	 ***************************************************************************/
   138	int esm_recv_pdn_connectivity_reject(nas_user_t *user, int pti, int ebi,
   139	                                     const pdn_connectivity_reject_msg *msg)
   140	{
   141	  LOG_FUNC_IN;
   142	
   143	  int esm_cause;
   144	
   145	  LOG_TRACE(INFO, "ESM-SAP   - Received PDN Connectivity Reject message "
   146	            "(pti=%d, ebi=%d, cause=%d)", pti, ebi, msg->esmcause);
   147	
   148	  /*
   149	   * Procedure transaction identity checking
   150	   */
   151	  if ( (pti == ESM_PT_UNASSIGNED) || esm_pt_is_reserved(pti) ) {
   152	    /* 3GPP TS 24.301, section 7.3.1, case a
   153	     * Reserved or unassigned PTI value
   154	     */
   155	    LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-SAP   - Invalid PTI value (pti=%d)", pti);
   156	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (ESM_CAUSE_INVALID_PTI_VALUE);
   157	  } else if ( esm_pt_is_not_in_use(user->esm_pt_data, pti) ) {
   158	    /* 3GPP TS 24.301, section 7.3.1, case a
   159	     * Assigned value that does not match any PTI in use
   160	     */
   161	    LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-SAP   - PTI mismatch (pti=%d)", pti);
   162	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (ESM_CAUSE_PTI_MISMATCH);
   163	  }
   164	  /*
   165	   * EPS bearer identity checking
   166	   */
   167	  else if ( (ebi != ESM_EBI_UNASSIGNED) || esm_ebr_is_reserved(user->esm_ebr_data, ebi) ) {
   168	    /* 3GPP TS 24.301, section 7.3.2, case a
   169	     * Assigned or reserved EPS bearer identity value */
   170	    LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "ESM-SAP   - Invalid EPS bearer identity (ebi=%d)",
   171	              ebi);
   172	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (ESM_CAUSE_INVALID_EPS_BEARER_IDENTITY);
   173	  }
   174	
   175	  /*
   176	   * Message processing
   177	   */
   178	  /* Get the ESM cause */
   179	  esm_cause = msg->esmcause;
   180	
   181	  /* Execute the PDN connectivity procedure not accepted by the network */
   182	  int rc = esm_proc_pdn_connectivity_reject(user, pti, &esm_cause);
   183	
   184	  if (rc != RETURNerror) {
   185	    esm_cause = ESM_CAUSE_SUCCESS;
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  /* Return the ESM cause value */
   189	  LOG_FUNC_RETURN (esm_cause);
   190	}

    60	static const char *_esm_pt_state_str[ESM_PT_STATE_MAX] = {
    61	  "PROCEDURE TRANSACTION INACTIVE",
    62	  "PROCEDURE TRANSACTION PENDING"
    63	};

    67	/* Return the index of the next available entry in the list of procedure
    68	 * transaction data */
    69	static int _esm_pt_get_available_entry(esm_pt_data_t *esm_pt_data);

    80	 **                                                                        **
    81	 ** Inputs:  None                                                      **
    82	 **      Others:    None                                       **

... [truncated] ...
 (0) otherwise.          **
   117	 **                                                                        **

   119	int esm_pt_assign(esm_pt_data_t *esm_pt_data)
   120	{
   121	  LOG_FUNC_IN;
   122	
   123	  /* Search for an available procedure transaction identity */
   124	  int i = _esm_pt_get_available_entry(esm_pt_data);
   125	
   126	  if (i < 0) {
   127	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (ESM_PT_UNASSIGNED);
   128	  }
   129	
   130	  /* Assign new procedure transaction */
   131	  esm_pt_data->context[i] =
   132	    (esm_pt_context_t *)malloc(sizeof(esm_pt_context_t));
   133	
   134	  if (esm_pt_data->context[i] == NULL) {
   135	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (ESM_PT_UNASSIGNED);
   136	  }
   137	
   138	  /* Store the index of the next available procedure transaction identity */
   139	  esm_pt_data->index = i + 1;
   140	
   141	  /* An available procedure transaction identity is found */
   142	  esm_pt_data->context[i]->pti = i + ESM_PTI_MIN;
   143	  /* Set the procedure transaction status to INACTIVE */
   144	  esm_pt_data->context[i]->status = ESM_PT_INACTIVE;
   145	  /* Disable the retransmission timer */
   146	  esm_pt_data->context[i]->timer.id = NAS_TIMER_INACTIVE_ID;
   147	  /* Setup retransmission timer parameters */
   148	  esm_pt_data->context[i]->args = NULL;
   149	
   150	  LOG_TRACE(INFO, "ESM-FSM   - Procedure transaction identity %d assigned",
   151	            esm_pt_data->context[i]->pti);
   152	  LOG_FUNC_RETURN (esm_pt_data->context[i]->pti);
   153	}

   155	/****************************************************************************
   156	 **                                                                        **
   157	 ** Name:    esm_pt_release()                                          **
   158	 **                                                                        **
   159	 ** Description: Release the given procedure transaction identity          **
   160	 **                                                                        **
   161	 ** Inputs:  pti:       The identity of the procedure transaction  **
   162	 **             to release                                 **
   163	 **      Others:    None                                       **
   164	 **                                                                        **
   165	 ** Outputs:     None                                                      **
   166	 **      Return:    RETURNok if the procedure transaction iden-**
   167	 **             tity has been successfully released;       **
   168	 **             RETURNerror otherwise.                     **
   169	 **                                                                        **
   170	 ***************************************************************************/
   171	int esm_pt_release(esm_pt_data_t *esm_pt_data, int pti)
   172	{
   173	  LOG_FUNC_IN;
   174	
   175	  if ( (pti < ESM_PTI_MIN) || (pti > ESM_PTI_MAX) ) {
   176	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (RETURNerror);
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  /* Get procedure transaction data */
   180	  esm_pt_context_t *ctx = esm_pt_data->context[pti - ESM_PTI_MIN];
   181	
   182	  if ( (ctx == NULL) || (ctx->pti != pti) ) {
   183	    /* Procedure transaction not assigned */
   184	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (RETURNerror);
   185	  }
   186	
   187	  /* Do not release active procedure transaction */
   188	  if (ctx->status != ESM_PT_INACTIVE) {
   189	    LOG_TRACE(ERROR, "ESM-FSM   - Procedure transaction is not INACTIVE");
   190	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (RETURNerror);
   191	  }
   192	
   193	  /* Stop the retransmission timer if still running */
   194	  if (ctx->timer.id != NAS_TIMER_INACTIVE_ID) {
   195	    LOG_TRACE(INFO, "ESM-FSM   - Stop retransmission timer %d",
   196	              ctx->timer.id);
   197	    ctx->timer.id = nas_timer_stop(ctx->timer.id);
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  /* Release the retransmisison timer parameters */

   100	/****************************************************************************
   101	 **                                                                        **
   102	 ** Name:    emm_esm_send()                                            **
   103	 **                                                                        **
   104	 ** Description: Processes the EMMESM Service Access Point primitive       **
   105	 **                                                                        **
   106	 ** Inputs:  msg:       The EMMESM-SAP primitive to process        **
   107	 **      Others:    None                                       **
   108	 **                                                                        **
   109	 ** Outputs:     None                                                      **
   110	 **      Return:    RETURNok, RETURNerror                      **
   111	 **      Others:    None                                       **
   112	 **                                                                        **
   113	 ***************************************************************************/
   114	int emm_esm_send(nas_user_t *user, const emm_esm_t *msg)
   115	{
   116	  LOG_FUNC_IN;
   117	
   118	  int rc = RETURNerror;
   119	  emm_esm_primitive_t primitive = msg->primitive;
   120	
   121	  LOG_TRACE(INFO, "EMMESM-SAP - Received primitive %s (%d)",
   122	            emm_esm_primitive2str(primitive - _EMMESM_START - 1), primitive);
   123	
   124	  switch (primitive) {
   125	
   126	  case _EMMESM_ESTABLISH_REQ:
   127	    /* ESM requests EMM to initiate an attach procedure before
   128	     * requesting subsequent connectivity to additional PDNs */
   129	    rc = emm_proc_attach_restart(user);
   130	    break;
   131	
   132	  case _EMMESM_ESTABLISH_CNF:
   133	
   134	    /* ESM notifies EMM that PDN connectivity procedure successfully
   135	     * processed */
   136	    if (msg->u.establish.is_attached) {
   137	      if (msg->u.establish.is_emergency) {
   138	        /* Consider the UE attached for emergency bearer services
   139	         * only */
   140	        rc = emm_proc_attach_set_emergency(user->emm_data);
   141	      }
   142	    } else {
   143	      /* Consider the UE locally detached from the network */
   144	      rc = emm_proc_attach_set_detach(user);
   145	    }
   146	
   147	    break;
   148	
   149	  case _EMMESM_ESTABLISH_REJ:
   150	    /* ESM notifies EMM that PDN connectivity procedure failed */
   151	    break;
   152	
   153	  case _EMMESM_UNITDATA_REQ:
   154	    /* ESM requests EMM to transfer ESM data unit to lower layer */
   155	    rc = lowerlayer_data_req(user, &msg->u.data.msg);
   156	    break;
   157	
   158	  default:
   159	    break;
   160	
   161	  }
   162	
   163	  if (rc != RETURNok) {
   164	    LOG_TRACE(WARNING, "EMMESM-SAP - Failed to process primitive %s (%d)",
   165	              emm_esm_primitive2str(primitive - _EMMESM_START - 1),
   166	              primitive);
   167	  }
   168	
   169	  LOG_FUNC_RETURN (rc);
   170	}
